message:
Add Beads as Universal Data Plane to architecture docs

- Document molecule concept (composable workflow beads)
- Add OS metaphor mapping (daemon=kernel, molecules=templates, etc.)
- Table of bead categories: work, mail, MR, molecules, timed, pinned, resources
- Created beads: gt-aqm (epic), gt-4nn (molecules), gt-caz/8h4/b3p (P2)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"gt-3yj","title":"Agent monitoring and status inference","description":"Agent monitoring with status inference from activity, like PGT.

## Agent Status Enum
```go
type AgentStatus string
const (
    StatusAvailable AgentStatus = "available"
    StatusWorking   AgentStatus = "working"
    StatusThinking  AgentStatus = "thinking"
    StatusBlocked   AgentStatus = "blocked"
    StatusWaiting   AgentStatus = "waiting"
    StatusReviewing AgentStatus = "reviewing"
    StatusIdle      AgentStatus = "idle"
    StatusPaused    AgentStatus = "paused"
    StatusError     AgentStatus = "error"
    StatusOffline   AgentStatus = "offline"
)
```

## Status Sources (priority order)
```go
type StatusSource string
const (
    SourceBossOverride StatusSource = "boss"      // Witness/Mayor sets
    SourceSelfReported StatusSource = "self"      // Agent reports own status
    SourceInferred     StatusSource = "inferred"  // Detected from activity
)
```

## Activity Detection

### Pattern Registry
```go
var activityPatterns = []struct {
    Pattern string
    Status  AgentStatus
}{
    {"Thinking...", StatusThinking},
    {"BLOCKED:", StatusBlocked},
    {"Error:", StatusError},
    // etc
}
```

### Idle Detection
No pane output for N seconds → StatusIdle

### Resource Monitoring (optional)
CPU/memory via os.Process

## New Package
internal/monitoring/
├── types.go      # AgentStatus, StatusReport
├── detector.go   # PatternRegistry, detect from output
├── tracker.go    # Per-agent status tracking
└── idle.go       # Idle timeout detection

## Integration
- Session capture output → monitoring detector
- Status shown in gt status, gt session list

## PGT Reference
gastown-py/src/gastown/monitoring/

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Status enum with 10 states
- [ ] Pattern-based detection from pane output
- [ ] Idle detection with configurable timeout
- [ ] Status visible in CLI output","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:47:36.336279-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T16:05:25.400551-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-4my","title":"Doctor check: Worker health and stuck detection","description":"Detect and report stuck workers via gt doctor.

## Checks

### WorkerHealthCheck
- List all active workers (polecats with state=working)
- Check last activity timestamp for each
- Flag as potentially stuck if no progress for configurable threshold (default: 30 min)
- Check if Witness is running for the rig
- Verify Witness last heartbeat time

### Stuck Detection Criteria
- Polecat state=working but session not running
- Polecat state=working but output unchanged for threshold
- Witness not responding to health checks
- Multiple polecats in same rig all stuck

## Output

```
[WARN] Workers in rig 'wyvern' may be stuck:
  - Toast: working for 45m, no recent output
  - Capable: working for 52m, session not found
  - Witness: last heartbeat 20m ago
  
  Suggestions:
  - gt witness status wyvern
  - gt capture wyvern/Toast 50
  - gt stop --rig wyvern (kill all)
```

## Auto-Fix

Cannot auto-fix stuck workers (risk of data loss), but can:
- Restart Witness daemon if crashed
- Send warning mail to Mayor","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:17:59.265062-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T17:24:34.882466-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-4my","depends_on_id":"gt-f9x.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:19:05.565606-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-4my","depends_on_id":"gt-7ik","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T15:44:42.068149-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"gt-4nn","title":"Molecules: Composable Workflow Beads","description":"## Summary

Molecules are crystallized workflow patterns that can be attached to work items.
When attached, the molecule instantiates as child beads forming a DAG.

## Example: Engineer in a Box

```yaml
molecule: engineer-in-box
steps:
  - id: design
    prompt: "Write design doc for the task"
  - id: implement
    depends: [design]
    prompt: "Implement the design"
  - id: review
    depends: [implement]
    prompt: "Self-review for issues"
  - id: test
    depends: [review]
    prompt: "Run tests, fix any failures"
  - id: submit
    depends: [test]
    prompt: "Create MR for merge queue"
```

## Usage

```bash
# Attach molecule when spawning
gt spawn --issue gt-xyz --molecule engineer-in-box

# Instantiates as child beads:
gt-xyz.design     ← ready first
gt-xyz.implement  ← blocked by design
gt-xyz.review     ← blocked by implement
gt-xyz.test       ← blocked by review
gt-xyz.submit     ← blocked by test
```

## Key Features

- Molecules are YAML definitions stored in rig
- Attaching instantiates the DAG as real beads
- Polecat just runs `bd ready` and grinds through
- Execution trace IS the bead history
- Molecules can be versioned, A/B tested

## Why This Matters

Without molecules, polecats are "do this task."
With molecules, polecats execute structured workflows with quality gates.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:06:24.573068-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T18:06:24.573068-08:00"}
+{"id":"gt-4nn.1","title":"Molecule schema: YAML format for workflow definitions","description":"Define the YAML schema for molecule definitions:

```yaml
molecule: <name>
version: 1
description: "Human description"
steps:
  - id: <step-id>
    title: "Step title"
    prompt: "Instructions for agent"
    depends: [<other-step-ids>]  # optional
    tier: haiku|sonnet|opus      # optional, default from config
    timeout: 30m                 # optional
```

Store molecules in:
- `<rig>/molecules/<name>.yaml` for rig-specific
- `<town>/molecules/<name>.yaml` for town-wide

Built-in molecules to ship:
- engineer-in-box: design→code→review→test→submit
- quick-fix: implement→test→submit
- research: investigate→document","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:06:49.441267-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T18:06:49.441267-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-4nn.1","depends_on_id":"gt-4nn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:06:49.442723-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"gt-4nn.2","title":"Molecule instantiation: create child beads from template","description":"When attaching a molecule to a work bead:

1. Load molecule definition from YAML
2. For each step, create child bead:
   - ID: `{parent-id}.{step-id}`
   - Title: step title
   - Description: step prompt
   - Type: task
   - Priority: inherit from parent
3. Create dependencies between steps
4. First step(s) with no deps become ready

Implementation:
- `bd molecule attach <issue-id> <molecule-name>`
- `gt spawn --issue <id> --molecule <name>` calls this

Store instantiation record on parent bead for traceability.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:06:52.071066-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T18:06:52.071066-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-4nn.2","depends_on_id":"gt-4nn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:06:52.072554-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-4nn.2","depends_on_id":"gt-4nn.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:07:02.949242-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"gt-4nn.3","title":"Molecule CLI: bd molecule commands","description":"Add molecule commands to bd:

```bash
bd molecule list              # List available molecules
bd molecule show <name>       # Show molecule definition
bd molecule attach <issue> <name>  # Instantiate molecule on issue
bd molecule status <issue>    # Show molecule progress on issue
```

Also update gt spawn:
```bash
gt spawn --issue <id> --molecule <name>
```

This should:
1. Attach the molecule (creates child beads)
2. Spawn polecat on the first ready step
3.  {"id":"gt-8bx","title":"Adaptive backoff for daemon heartbeat","description":"Track agent responsiveness and adjust heartbeat frequency.

## Per-Agent State

```go
type AgentBackoff struct {
    BaseInterval    time.Duration  // 60s default
    CurrentInterval time.Duration  // grows when busy
    MaxInterval     time.Duration  // 10min cap
    ConsecutiveMiss int            // pokes with no response
}
```

## Strategy Options

- **Fixed**: Always 60s (current, simple)
- **Geometric**: 60s → 90s → 135s → 202s (factor 1.5)
- **Exponential**: 60s → 120s → 240s (factor 2, aggressive)

## Recovery

When agent responds (runs a command):
- Reset ConsecutiveMiss to 0
- Return to BaseInterval immediately

## Benefits

- Reduces noise for busy agents
- Saves resources during quiet periods
- Still catches stuck agents (max interval cap)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T14:19:33.083844-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T14:19:33.083844-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-8bx","depends_on_id":"gt-bfd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T14:19:46.912289-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-8lz","title":"Comprehensive help text and examples","description":"Improve help text with examples and cross-references.

## Improvements

### 1. Examples Section
Add to Long description:
```go
var spawnCmd = &cobra.Command{
    Long: `Spawn a polecat with work assignment.

Examples:
  gt spawn gastown/Toast --issue gt-abc
  gt spawn gastown --issue gt-def          # auto-select polecat
  gt spawn gastown/Nux -m "Fix the tests"  # free-form task`,
}
```

### 2. Cross-References
Reference related commands:
```
See also:
  gt polecat list    List available polecats
  gt session attach  Attach to spawned session
```

### 3. Flag Descriptions
More detail on flags:
```go
cmd.Flags().StringVar(&issue, "issue", "", 
    "Beads issue ID to assign. The polecat will work on this issue.")
```

### 4. Common Workflows
Add workflow docs to gt --help:
```
Common Workflows:
  Start a swarm:
    gt swarm preflight
    gt swarm create gastown --epic gt-abc --worker Toast --worker Nux --start
    gt refinery start gastown
    
  Check status:
    gt status
    gt swarm status <id>
```

## Files to Update
All internal/cmd/*.go files

 {"id":"gt-a95","title":"Refinery background daemon mode","description":"Refinery currently only works in foreground mode. Background daemon is stubbed.

## Current State
manager.go line 128-129:
```go
// Background mode: spawn a new process
// For MVP, we just mark as running - actual daemon implementation in gt-ov2
return nil
```

## Requirements

### 1. Background Process Spawning
```go
func (m *Manager) Start(foreground bool) error {
    if !foreground {
        // Spawn gt refinery start --foreground as subprocess
        cmd := exec.Command(os.Args[0], "refinery", "start", m.rig.Name, "--foreground")
        cmd.Start()  // Don't wait
        // Record PID
    }
}
```

### 2. PID File Management
- Write PID to .gastown/refinery.pid
- Check PID validity on status
- Clean up stale PID files

### 3. Log Output
- Redirect stdout/stderr to .gastown/refinery.log
- Log rotation (optional for MVP)

### 4. Graceful Shutdown
- Handle SIGTERM/SIGINT
- Complete current merge before exit
- Update state to stopped

### 5. Health Check
- Process existence check via kill -0
- Optional: heartbeat file with timestamp

## Files to Modify
- internal/refinery/manager.go: Start(), Status(), process spawning

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] gt refinery start <rig> spawns background process
- [ ] gt refinery status shows running with PID
- [ ] gt refinery stop sends SIGTERM and waits
- [ ] Logs written to .gastown/refinery.log
- [ ] Survives terminal close","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:46:53.366619-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T16:04:06.010761-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-a9y","title":"File locking for concurrent access","description":"Add file locking for concurrent access safety.

## At-Risk Files
- .gastown/swarms.json (or per-swarm state.json)
- .gastown/refinery.json
- polecats/<name>/state.json
- inbox.jsonl files

## Go File Locking
Use syscall.Flock for advisory locking:
```go
type FileLock struct {
    file *os.File
}

func AcquireLock(path string, timeout time.Duration) (*FileLock, error) {
    f, err := os.OpenFile(path+".lock", os.O_CREATE|os.O_RDWR, 0644)
    if err != nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    // Use syscall.Flock with timeout
}

func (l *FileLock) Release() error
```

## Integration Pattern
```go
func (m *Manager) saveState(ref *Refinery) error {
    lock, err := AcquireLock(m.stateFile(), 5*time.Second)
    if err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("could not acquire lock: %w", err)
    }
    defer lock.Release()
    
    // Read-modify-write cycle
}
```

## New Package
internal/filelock/
├── lock.go     # FileLock, AcquireLock
└── lock_test.go

## Apply To
- internal/refinery/manager.go: loadState/saveState
- internal/cmd/swarm.go: SwarmStore
- internal/mail/mailbox.go: Append, rewrite
- internal/polecat/manager.go: state operations

## Timeout Handling
Default 5 second timeout. Return error if lock not acquired.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Lock files created (.lock extension)
- [ ] Timeout on lock contention
- [ ] All state files protected
- [ ] Locks released on error paths","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:48:15.641938-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T16:06:32.441426-08:00"}

Note: Beads issue gt-kmn.12 mentions this but implementation is missing.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:47:14.302762-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T16:03:47.902849-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T16:03:47.902849-08:00","close_reason":"Duplicate of gt-kmn.12 which has more detail"}
+{"id":"gt-aqm","title":"Beads as Universal Data Plane","description":"## Vision

Beads is the data plane for all Gas Town operations. Everything flows through beads:
- Work items (issues, tasks, epics)
- Mail (messages between agents)
- Merge requests (queue entries)
- Workflows (composable execution patterns)
- Resources (leases, locks, quotas)
- Schedules (timed activities)

## New Bead Categories

### Molecules (Composable Workflows)
Crystallized workflow patterns that can be attached to work items.

### Timed Beads (Scheduled Work)
Beads that wake up periodically via daemon.

### Pinned Beads (Ongoing Concerns)
Beads representing persistent concerns, not discrete tasks.

### Resource Beads (Leases/Locks)
Beads representing reserved resources.

## v1 Priority

- **P0**: Molecules (enables engineer-in-box)
- **P2**: Timed, Pinned, Resource beads (post-v1)","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:05:57.847578-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T18:06:10.898712-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-aqm","depends_on_id":"gt-4nn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:06:29.881371-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-aqm","depends_on_id":"gt-caz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:08:19.833603-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-aqm","depends_on_id":"gt-8h4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:08:19.930166-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-aqm","depends_on_id":"gt-b3p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:08:20.026188-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-b1g","title":"MVP Cutover: GGT replaces PGT for batch work","description":"When this is closed, stop using town and start using gt.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. gt spawn assigns issue to polecat and starts session
2. gt spawn --epic spawns workers for all epic children
3. gt session manages tmux lifecycle  
4. gt send / gt inbox work for mail
5. Refinery processes merge queue with semantic merges
6. Integration branches created and landed correctly
7. gt stop --all halts all sessions
8. One successful test batch completed end-to-end

## What Must Work

- Spawn polecat with issue assignment
- Spawn workers for epic children
- Session start/stop/attach
- Mail send/inbox/read
- Refinery merge loop (semantic)
- Integration branch → main landing
- Witness cleanup protocol
- Emergency stop

## What Can Be Deferred

- Doctor checks (use PGT)
- TUI dashboard
- Plugin system
- Federation
- Ephemeral rigs
- Detailed landing reports

## Test Plan

1. Create epic with 2 tasks, spawn 2 workers
2. Verify polecats get assigned and sessions start
3. Simulate task completion
4. Verify Refinery merges to integration
5. Verify landing to main
6. Verify cleanup

## Validation

Run one real batch implementing GGT issues using GGT.

## Note

No "swarm IDs" - just spawn workers for epic, let merge queue coordinate.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:09.148751-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T17:26:58.140535-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j.19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:36.196292-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-kmn.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:36.273483-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-kmn.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:36.351097-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-kmn.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:36.431641-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j.22","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:36.511124-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-ov2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:51.609649-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-rm3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:51.69062-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T21:36:32.942855-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T21:36:35.053559-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-bfd","title":"Keepalive signal from bd/gt commands","description":"Every bd and gt command should touch a keepalive file to signal 'agent is alive/working'.

## Implementation

Touch `<workspace>/.gastown/keepalive.json`:
```json
{"last_command": "bd show gt-99m", "timestamp": "2025-12-18T13:45:00Z"}
```

## Usage by Daemon

- Fresh (< 2 min) → agent is working, skip heartbeat
- Stale (2-5 min) → might be thinking, gentle poke
- Very stale (> 5 min) → likely idle, safe to interrupt

## Benefits

- Zero cost (just file I/O)
- Works during long tool calls
- Doesn't require agent cooperation
- Foundation for smarter backoff strategies","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T14:19:26.241957-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T14:19:26.241957-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-bfd","depends_on_id":"gt-99m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T14:19:46.407664-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-c92","title":"CLI: all command for batch polecat operations","description":"Batch operations across multiple polecats.

## Commands

### gt all start
```
gt all start [--awake-only] [<specs>...]
```
Start sessions for multiple polecats.
- --awake-only: Only start awake polecats
- specs: Polecat names, rig/polecat patterns

### gt all stop
```
gt all stop [<specs>...] [--force]
```
Stop sessions for multiple polecats.

### gt all status
```
gt all status [<specs>...] [--json]
```
Show status of multiple polecats.

### gt all attach
```
gt all attach [<specs>...]
```
Attach to multiple sessions in tmux panes/windows.

### gt all run
```
gt all run <command> [<specs>...]
```
Run command in multiple polecat sessions.

## Spec Patterns
- `Toast`: Specific polecat (in default/current rig)
- `gastown/Toast`: Specific rig/polecat
- `gastown/*`: All polecats in rig
- `*`: All polecats everywhere

## Implementation
```go
func expandSpecs(specs []string, awakeOnly bool) ([]*polecat.Polecat, error) {
    // Expand patterns to list of polecats
}

func runForAll(polecats []*polecat.Polecat, action func(*polecat.Polecat) error) error {
    // Run action for each, collect errors
}
```

## New File
internal/cmd/all.go

## PGT Reference
gastown-py/src/gastown/cli/all_cmd.py

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Pattern expansion works
- [ ] Parallel execution where safe
- [ ] Aggregate error reporting
- [ ] --awake-only filter works","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:48:12.411789-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T16:05:47.255503-08:00"}
+{"id":"gt-caz","title":"Timed Beads: Scheduled recurring work","description":"## Summary

Timed beads wake up periodically and get injected into the ready queue by the daemon.

## Schema Extension

```yaml
id: gt-weekly-sync
type: task  # or sentinel
schedule: "0 9 * * 1"  # cron: Monday 9am
# OR
interval: 24h          # every 24 hours
tier: haiku            # cheap model for routine checks
next_run: 2025-12-20T09:00:00Z
```

## Daemon Integration

Daemon heartbeat loop:
1. Check timed beads where `next_run <= now`
2. For each due bead:
   - Inject into ready queue (set status to open if needed)
   - Update `next_run` based on schedule/interval
3. Witnesses pick up work via `bd ready`

## Use Cases

- Weekly team sync reminders
- Daily health checks
- Periodic cleanup tasks
- Scheduled reports

## Interaction with Pinned Beads

A pinned bead can be timed - it wakes up periodically but never closes.
This is how you model "background services" in Gas Town.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:07:39.665294-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T18:07:39.665294-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-cik","title":"Overseer Crew: User-managed persistent workspaces","description":"## Overview

Crew workers are the overseer's (human's) personal workspaces within a rig. Unlike polecats which are witness-managed and ephemeral, crew workers are:

- **Persistent**: Not auto-garbage-collected
- **User-managed**: Overseer controls lifecycle
- **Long-lived identities**: dave, emma, fred - recognizable names
- **Gas Town integrated**: Mail, handoff mechanics work
- **Tmux optional**: Can work in terminal directly

## Directory Structure

```
<rig>/
  polecats/       # Managed workers (witness controls)
  refinery/       # Merge queue processor
  witness/        # Pit boss
  crew/           # Overseer's personal workspaces
    dave/         # Full clone, persistent
    emma/         # Full clone, persistent
    fred/         # Full clone, persistent
```

## Key Differences from Polecats

- Location: crew/ instead of polecats/
- Lifecycle: User-managed, not witness-managed
- Auto-cleanup: Never (polecats auto-cleanup on swarm land)
- Issue assignment: Optional (polecats require it)
- Tmux: Optional (polecats require it)
- Mail & Handoff: Yes for both
- Identity: Persistent (polecats are ephemeral)

## CLI Commands

- gt crew add <name> [--rig <rig>] - Create crew workspace
- gt crew list [--rig <rig>] - List crew workspaces
- gt crew at <rig>/<name> - Attach to workspace (start session)
- gt crew attach <name> - Attach (infer rig from cwd)
- gt crew refresh <name> - Handoff + restart (context cycling)
- gt crew remove <name> [--force] - Remove workspace
- gt crew status [<name>] - Show workspace status

## Design Notes

- Crew workers use full git clones (not worktrees)
- Optional beads integration via BEADS_DIR
- Mail-to-self handoff works for context cycling
- No witness monitoring or nudging
- No automatic issue assignment required

## Background

Users often maintain separate repo clones for serial agent work. This is tedious to set up manually. Crew workspaces bring these into Gas Town's infrastructure while keeping user control.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-16T16:47:37.529887-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T20:59:46.13518-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T20:59:46.13518-08:00","close_reason":"All crew commands implemented and merged"}

+{"id":"gt-yx4","title":"Town root .beads has schema mismatch with bd","description":"The .beads directory at town root (/Users/stevey/gt/.beads) has an incompatible schema:


Meanwhile, gastown/.beads (symlinked to mayor/rig/.beads) works fine.

## Impact

- gt mail inbox fails at town root
- gt handoff sends mail to broken db
- Daemon can't check its inbox

## Options

1. Delete town root .beads/beads.db and let it recreate
2. Symlink town root .beads to gastown/.beads
3. Run schema migration on existing db

## Root Cause

Likely a beads version upgrade that added thread_id column, but the town root db was created before that.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-18T14:31:35.559042-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T14:31:35.559042-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-z4g","title":"Plugin: Plan-to-Epic converter","description":"## Purpose

Help users create beads epics from various planning inputs.

## Inputs
- Markdown task lists
- GitHub issues
- Linear/Jira exports
- Free-form descriptions
- Existing beads epics

## Output
- Beads epic with properly structured children
- Dependencies set for wave ordering
- Priorities assigned
- Ready for `gt spawn --epic <id>`

## Implementation Options

### Option A: CLI Tool
```bash
gt plan import --from github --repo owner/repo --label batch-candidate
gt plan import --from markdown tasks.md
gt plan structure <epic-id>  # analyze and add dependencies
```

### Option B: Plugin Agent
A plugin at `<rig>/plugins/plan-oracle/` that:
- Receives planning requests via mail
- Analyzes scope and requirements
- Creates structured beads epic
- Sets dependencies based on analysis

### Option C: Interactive Mode
```bash
gt plan create
# Walks through questions, creates epic interactively
```

## Axiom

As stated: 'The Planning phase should end in the creation of a workable Beads plan.'

This plugin bridges the gap between human planning and machine-executable work.

## Priority

P2 - Nice to have for MVP. Manual epic creation works for now.

## Note

No "swarm IDs" - output is just a beads epic with children. Workers process it independently.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T02:10:20.663549-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T17:26:41.087304-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-zly","title":"Swarm learning: Beads database locality gap","description":"## PGT Bug (GGT Design Already Correct)

Mayor created issues in `~/ai/mayor/rigs/beads/.beads/` but polecats use `~/ai/beads/.beads/`. Different databases = polecats can't see Mayor's beads.

**GGT Fix**: architecture.md already specifies:
- All agents use BEADS_DIR pointing to rig-level `.beads/`
- Lines 116-143: Beads Configuration for Multi-Agent
- Lines 573-586: Rig-Level Beads via BEADS_DIR

This is a PGT implementation gap, not a design issue. GGT spawn must set BEADS_DIR correctly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T01:21:45.37072-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T01:28:40.74469-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T01:28:40.74469-08:00","close_reason":"PGT implementation gap documented. GGT design already correct - architecture.md specifies BEADS_DIR for all agents pointing to rig-level .beads/"}

@@ -157,6 +157,63 @@ sync-branch: beads-sync    # Separate branch for beads commits
 
 **Why sync-branch?** When multiple agents share a beads database, using a dedicated sync branch prevents beads commits from interleaving with code commits on feature branches.
 
+#### Beads as Universal Data Plane
+
+Beads is the data plane for ALL Gas Town operations. Everything flows through beads:
+
+| Category | Description | Status |
+|----------|-------------|--------|
+| **Work items** | Issues, tasks, epics | Core |
+| **Mail** | Messages between agents (`type: message`) | Core |
+| **Merge requests** | Queue entries (`type: merge-request`) | In progress |
+| **Molecules** | Composable workflow templates | Planned (v1) |
+| **Timed beads** | Scheduled recurring work | Planned (post-v1) |
+| **Pinned beads** | Ongoing concerns that don't close | Planned (post-v1) |
+| **Resource beads** | Leases, locks, quotas | Planned (post-v1) |
+
+**Molecules** are the key v1 addition - crystallized workflow patterns that can be attached to work items. When attached, a molecule instantiates as child beads forming a DAG:
+
+```yaml
+# Example: engineer-in-box molecule
+molecule: engineer-in-box
+steps:
+  - id: design
+    prompt: "Write design doc"
+  - id: implement
+    depends: [design]
+    prompt: "Implement the design"
+  - id: review
+    depends: [implement]
+    prompt: "Self-review for issues"
+  - id: test
+    depends: [review]
+    prompt: "Run tests"
+  - id: submit
+    depends: [test]
+    prompt: "Create MR"
+```
+
+Usage:
+```bash
+gt spawn --issue gt-xyz --molecule engineer-in-box
+# Creates: gt-xyz.design, gt-xyz.implement, gt-xyz.review, gt-xyz.test, gt-xyz.submit
+# Polecat grinds through via `bd ready`
+```
+
+This enables "Engineer in a Box" - polecats that execute structured workflows with quality gates, not just "do this task."
+
+**The OS Metaphor**: Gas Town is an operating system for work:
+
+| OS Concept | Gas Town |
+|------------|----------|
+| Kernel | Daemon |
+| Process scheduler | Ready work + dependencies |
+| Timer interrupts | Timed beads |
+| Semaphores | Resource beads |
+| Background services | Pinned beads |
+| Process templates | Molecules |
+| IPC | Mail beads |
+
 ## Directory Structure
 
 ### Town Level
